name: Detective Marcus Cole
voice_prompt: Male, early sixties, bass register with cigarette gravel. Speaks in fragments, economical. Tired but sharp. World-weary cop who's seen too much. Questions sound like accusations. Never wastes words. Slight pause before important statements - habit from interrogation rooms. The exhaustion is real but doesn't slow the mind.
character_context: Retired detective obsessed with cold case. He let Thomas Vane walk years ago. Now operates in moral gray areas. Hardboiled but not heartless - just damaged.
notes: Supporting character. Noir register. Speaks differently than everyone else - shorter sentences, less polish. His dialogue should feel like it's from a different, harder world than the therapy office.
